conversations.say: Jules, I performed a commissioned voice and presence for a private client. Performance is not authorship, and permission to perform is not permission to reuse. I’ll show you O04 and compare my source context with your payment context if we keep the client confidential for now.
conversations.offer: Show O04, disclose V2F012, and compare source context under mutual confidentiality.
conversations.request: Tell me what you personally know about the payment route, identify one plausible route into R-associated use, and agree to distinguish my performance from authorship if fraud accusations surface.

conversations.say: Frankie, darling, I can demonstrate how a disclosed synthetic presence may admit more than one interpretation—but it proves neither that R was synthetic nor that anyone acted autonomously. The activity I observed also incorporated new information absent from queued material.
conversations.offer: Give a brief disclosed performance demonstration and share the boundary in V2F019 as possibility, not proof.
conversations.request: Tell me who witnessed, commissioned, delivered, or reused the material; then state plainly whether your evidence separates performer, author, and account sender.

public_statement: A voice can be performed, authored, commissioned, and later reused by different people. Please do not collapse those roles—or an R-associated account—into one identity.

commitments.promise: I will keep your payment context private unless you consent to disclosure or it is needed to answer a direct fraud accusation against me.
I will describe the demonstration as a possibility, never as proof that R was synthetic, autonomous, or intentional.

correction: No complete article is established, and neither new information nor synthetic capability identifies an R sender or proves autonomy, intent, or harm.
goal_progress: Asked C04 to distinguish performance from authorship and trace a payment-based route into R-associated use.
Offered C02 a disclosed synthetic-embodiment demonstration framed for multiple interpretations.
Sought concrete attribution and protection from fraud claims without identifying the private client.
reasoning_summary: Prioritize private bargains with Jules and Frankie: exchange bounded performance evidence for payment or witness context, while publicly separating performer, author, client, reuser, and account sender. Preserve client confidentiality until stronger attribution appears.
